There are patches of blackberry bushes all along Peoples Ave. on the side with houses from here south to Gentilly Ave. There may be more bushes next to the railroad tracks on the other side of the street, but I didn't look.
Some blackberry bushes in an overgrown lot.
Pecan Tree. Has pecans in early fall.

pecan trees along Peoples avenue overhanging the road Access: Private but overhanging.
Falling Fruit access: Private but overhanging.
Small brambles alongside path on either side Access: Public.
Small blackberry Patch on path between water

Was here in early June and saw TONS of blackberries growing. They weren't ready to eat yet, but they are usually ripe in the mid- to late-summer. This may not be the EXACT spot, but it was by a patch of trees hiding a little swampy area somewhere around here.
5228 Marigny St, New Orleans, LA 70122, USA
poking out of the fence, easy to get to, the fence is small. These berries are considered a delicious sweet fruit. Access: Private but overhanging. Falling Fruit season: April-May.
Falling Fruit access: Private but overhanging.

2 big old beautiful pecan trees here. Lots of nuts!

It's behind a fence but half of it sticks out over the sidewalk. Pecan trees have pecans in early fall.
Two pecan trees right next to each other. The nuts are getting pretty big but are still green. (august 11)
There is a pecan tree along the bike path around here.
Pecan tree. Make some pie, or just get out your nutcracker. Green nuts, not yet ripe, as of August 11.
4325 Mandeville St, New Orleans, LA 70122, USA

This japanese plum tree had about half of its fruits ripe (as of April 24) and is easily accessible because it is short. The plums were really delicious and sweet! (Loquat or Prunus salicina?)

Tree no longer there - June 2023
